problem.statement: Service areas, operational zones, SLA, capacity pressure, pauses and fulfillment modes must produce one deterministic DSH decision consumed by cart, checkout, order and dispatch instead of independent surface assumptions.
problem.currentWorkaround: Zones, SLA and capacity can be edited, but assignment SLA, pause state, delivery-mode eligibility, operational effects, audit readback and rollback are not represented as one governed journey.

forbiddenActions: change policy without reason and idempotency
hide a capacity or serviceability denial

actors.role: Authenticated customer receiving serviceability and fulfillment eligibility
forbiddenActions: continue checkout after a canonical denial

actors.role: Authorized store operator consuming store-scoped policy readback
forbiddenActions: enable BThwani delivery without operator policy

permittedActions: receive assignments allowed by effective policy
forbiddenActions: receive dispatch while the effective mode or zone is paused

surfaces.exclusionReason: Field visits consume service-area readiness through JRN-024 and do not mutate or execute JRN-029 operational routing decisions.

outcome.statement: Every affected surface receives one versioned DSH operational decision that combines active zone, SLA, capacity pause and pressure, fulfillment mode and service-area truth, with explicit effects for cart, checkout, order and dispatch.
outcome.baselineState: Editing primitives exist without a complete decision, impact and rollback model.
outcome.targetState: One governed decision and audit lifecycle is used across all required surfaces.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted contract, database, backend, frontend, runtime and independent product review

criteria: Assignment SLA is represented alongside preparation and delivery SLA.
Capacity supports explicit pause and resume with a reason and version guard.
BThwani delivery, partner delivery and client pickup are independently governed per zone.
Evaluation fails closed for inactive zones, paused capacity, disabled modes and exhausted pressure thresholds.
The decision returns explicit cart, checkout, order and dispatch effects.
Every mutation is authorized, idempotent, correlated, versioned and audited.
Audit snapshots can rollback only the same reversible aggregate.
The control panel exposes loading, empty, success, conflict, forbidden and recovery states.
Affected client, partner and captain integrations consume the canonical decision rather than local truth.
The onboarding-fee definition remains DSH-owned and creates no WLT balance or ledger mutation.

business: DSH owns operational policy and serviceability decisions.
Service-area geofences remain the spatial boundary truth.
Every affected workflow consumes the same decision version.
Policy history is append-only and rollback creates a new version.
negative: No frontend derives serviceability from local constants.
No disabled or paused path creates checkout or dispatch success.
No stale expected version overwrites newer policy.
No DSH policy mutation writes WLT financial truth.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-029 code, data, contract, impact and verification gaps without deploying production or changing WLT ownership.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
No PR merge force push release or production deployment is authorized.
Service-area geofences remain the spatial truth owner.
Independent approvals cannot be self-issued by engineering.
